observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
direct the (a) MA to (i)
consistently record inventories
and semi-expendable properties
to their respective asset accounts
upon purchase; and (ii) ensure
that the recognition to expense
of inventories and semi-
expendable properties is
supported with sufficient
supporting documents; and (b)
GSO to (i) prepare and submit
SSMI to the MAO; (ii) maintain
RegSPI for proper monitoring of
semi-expendable properties; and
(iii) conduct physical count of
inventories and semi-
expendable properties and
submit to the MAO and AT on
semestral basis the RPCI and
RPCSP using the prescribed
format.

We recommended that the LCE
(a) direct the MA to reclassify
the procured PPE as well as
major repairs and improvement
to the appropriate asset
accounts; (b) direct the GSO
Head to (i) forward the related
donation documents of the
ambulance used by RHU to the
MAO for proper recording; (ii)
cause the preparation of the
IIRUP to facilitate the disposal
of unserviceable PPE; and (iii)
ensure that all motor vehicles
owned by the Municipality are
registered with the LTO and
with updated GSIS insurance;
(c) compel the Inventory
Committee to prioritize the
conduct of physical count of
PPE and submission of the
RPCPPE to the AT; and (d)
consider establishing a
Municipal Titling Committee
responsible for validating
parcels of land owned by the
Municipality and facilitating the
transfer of titles under its name.

We recommended that the LCE
(a) direct the MA to (i) exert
more efforts to determine the
significant details of long
outstanding balances in the Due
to NGAs account; (ii) confirm
the accuracy of the balances of
unliquidated fund transfers by
sending confirmation letters,
coordinating with the source
agencies, and returning
unutilized balances, if required;
and (iii) file a request to COA
for authority to write off
dormant fund transfers after the
conditions and requirements as
per COA Circular No. 2016-005
have been fully satisfied; and
(b) continue to satisfactorily
implement the PPAs funded by
NGAs and promptly return the
unutilized balances to their
respective sources.

We recommended that the LCE
require the (a) MDRRMC to
fully implement PPAs focused
on disaster prevention,
mitigation, and preparedness at
the set time to avoid losses from
hazards as well as to reduce the
intensity of future disasters; (b)
MDRRMO Head to desist from
incurring ineligible expenditures
charged against the DRRMF to
serve the intended purpose of
the fund; (c) BAC Secretariat to
(i) post in the PhilGEPS the
RFQ and Notice of Award for
the procurements covered by the
alternative mode of procurement
– Shopping; (d) MA, BAC
Chairperson, and MDRRMO
Head to submit the documentary
requirements, unsubmitted DVs;
and (e) MA to (i) submit in a
timely manner the Report on
Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF; and (ii) ensure that the
necessary documents supporting
the claims are valid and
complete before payment is
made to suppliers.

We recommended that the LCE
(a) exhaust all possible means to
enforce the collection of real
property tax delinquencies,
including public awareness
campaigns, and streamlining
process, among others; and (b)
coordinate with the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) and
the Local Finance Committee
(LFC) members to review
existing local policies and
regulations, identify areas for
improvement. and implement
reforms to strengthen local tax
administration.

We reiterated our prior years’
audit recommendations that the
LCE require the (a) BAC
Secretariat to submit to the AT
the copies of approved
construction contracts and POs
together with their supporting
documents within five working
days from the execution thereof
to facilitate review and other
necessary audit procedures
thereon; and (b) GSO to give
prompt notice and submit to the
AT the delivery receipts on the
schedule of deliveries within 24
hours from acceptance of the
purchased items.

We recommended that the LCE
require the MA to (a) review the
amounts of taxes withheld and
remitted/posted to ensure
correctness of data and avoid
discrepancies (b) immediately
remit to the BIR the unremitted
taxes withheld in the amount of
₱140,237.61; and (c) practice
intact remittance of taxes
withheld to the BIR within the
prescribed period to avoid
incurring surcharges and
penalties.

We recommended that the LCE
require the GFPS – TWG to (a)
execute their task to catalyze
and accelerate gender
mainstreaming in all programs
and projects of the Municipality;
and (b) expedite the
implementation of HGDG to
fully comply with the provisions
of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC Circular No. 2013-01, as
amended by JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended that the LCE
require the GFPS – TWG to (a)
execute their task to catalyze
and accelerate gender
mainstreaming in all programs
and projects of the Municipality;
and (b) expedite the
implementation of HGDG to
fully comply with the provisions
of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC Circular No. 2013-01, as
amended by JMC No. 2016-01.

1.5. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the
MT and MA to: (a) consistently adhere to the guidelines for reporting and accounting
of collections and deposits under existing regulations; and (b) continuously
collaborate and reconcile their respective records to promptly identify and rectify any
errors.

2.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA to continuously
comply with the timely preparation of BRS and adjustment of the reconciling items
for the fair presentation of the CIB accounts as of year-end.

3.18 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the Inventory Committee
to perform a complete physical count of all properties owned and controlled by the
Municipality and reconcile the results with the accounting records; (b) require the
GSO to maintain PCs and LRNPC; (c) instruct the Chairman of the Inventory
Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Engineering Office to
prepare and submit the a complete inventory of all Local Roads, the corresponding
Road Map and the RPCLRN; and (d) direct the MA to (i) draw the necessary entries
for the noted misclassifications, non-capitalization and non-recognition of PPE for
accurate presentation of the account in the financial statements; (ii) maintain
complete PPELCs, including LRNLC; and (iii) provide the required disclosure in the
NFS for the Road Network account.

4.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to recognize the
accrued liability from earned leave benefits at year-end; and (b) HRMO Officer to
regularly furnish the MAO a summary or report on earned leave credits.

5.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) recognize the
appropriate asset, revenue, and expense accounts in the TF books upon receipt and
utilization of funds from other agencies arising from non-exchange transactions; (b)
record the transfer of assets in both the TF and GF books; and (c) restate the
Comparative Statement of Financial Performance of the ensuing year to reflect the
recognition of revenues and expenses.

6.9. We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct all implementing
departments to (i) closely monitor the implementation of programs and projects to
ensure the timely implementation/completion thereof; and (ii) coordinate with the
MTO and MAO for the prompt return of excess balances upon project completion;
and (b) direct the MA to (i) maintain proper accounting practices on the recording of
receipt and utilization of fund transfers; (ii) submit a copy of the APMV to the Audit
Team for review; and (iii) make the necessary adjustments to accurately record the
procured vehicles.

7.15. We recommended and the LCE agreed (a) as the head of the Municipal
Development Council, to direct its members to (i) continue adhering to the proper
allocation and utilization of the 20% DF; (ii) ensure implementation and completion
of all programmed PPAs within set timeline to ensure the prompt realization of
benefits for the intended beneficiaries; and (iii) prioritize the utilization of the DF’s
continuing appropriation through the implementation of intended projects or
reallocation to other priority development initiatives; (b) to direct the (i) OIC-MEO
to ensure that all infrastructure projects are implemented in accordance with the time
stipulated in the contract agreements and that any negative slippage is promptly
addressed and resolved accordingly; and (ii) MA to impose liquidated damages for
the remaining delayed projects.

8.17 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) MDRRMC to fully
implement PPAs focused on disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness within
the prescribed timeframe to minimize hazard-related losses and reduce the impact of
future disasters; (b) BAC Secretariat to (i) post in the PhilGEPS, the RFQ and Notice
of Award for procurements conducted through alternative mode of procurement –
Shopping; (c) MA, BAC Chairperson, and MDRRMO Officer to ensure that all
necessary documents supporting the claims are valid, complete, and properly
reviewed before processing payment to suppliers; (d) MA to (i) record the necessary
journal entries to recognize the PPE totaling ₱19,130,376.87 in the GF books; and (ii)
henceforth, observe proper recording of transactions under TL-DRRMF; and (e)
MDRRMO to submit in a timely manner the Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF.

9.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the HRMO to (a) conduct
review and evaluation of the Municipality’s existing organizational structure to
properly identify and establish the appropriate human resource complement; (b)
ensure that the hiring of JO/COS personnel is based on necessity of services,
qualifications and performance; and (c) to strictly enforce the use of biometrics
system and ensure that payrolls at period-end are supported by biometrics-generated
DTRs.

10.10 We reiterated the prior year recommendation that the LCE (a) exhaust all
possible means to enforce the collection of real property tax delinquencies, including
public awareness campaigns, and streamlining process, among others; and (b)
coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Finance Committee members
to review existing local policies and regulations, identify areas for improvement and
implement reforms to strengthen local tax administration.
10.11 We also recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MTO to provide
the MAO a copy of Certified List of All Property Tax Delinquencies as of year-end;
and (b) MAO to make the necessary adjustments on the RPT and SET receivable
accounts.

11.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GFPS to (a) prioritize
the implementation of the identified PPAs as per GPB to achieve and promote
desirable gender responsive society; (b) utilize fully the GAD budget through
implementation of GAD responsive PPAS to attain the objectives for which the funds
were appropriated; (c) exert efforts to come up with a database on sex-disaggregated
data or perform gender analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related
PPAs are not based on incorrect assumptions and stereotypes; (d) initiate the
capacitation of key members of the GFPS on the use of HGDG tool to guarantee the
integration of gender concerns in the design, management, implementation,
monitoring and evaluation of the PPAs and to determine the efficiency and
effectiveness of the LGU’s mainstreaming efforts; and (e) create a Monitoring and
Evaluation Team to evaluate the outcomes of the GAD PPAs.

12.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the
amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/posted to ensure correctness of data and
avoid discrepancies; (b) and practice intact remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR
within the prescribed period to avoid the incurring surcharges and penalties to be
charged against the erring employees.

13.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the HRMO Officer and MA
to continue to comply with Section 6b of RA No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997, Section
3 Rule VII of RA No. 9679 or the HDMF Law of 2009 and Section 18b of the Revised
IRR of RA No. 7875 as amended by RA No. 9241 and RA No. 10606 otherwise known
as the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and its RIRR on the withholding and
full remittance of employee premiums/contributions within the prescribed period.

14.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA and MT to settle
expeditiously the outstanding suspensions and disallowances and be more prudent in
the exercise of their functions to minimize, if not eliminate, audit suspensions,
disallowances and charges.

We recommended that the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) instruct
the MT and MA to (a)
consistently adhere to the
guidelines for reporting and
accounting of collections and
deposits under existing
regulations; and (b)
continuously collaborate and
reconcile their respective
records to promptly identify and
rectify any errors.

We recommended that the LCE
direct the MA to (a) strictly
comply with the requirements (a) Implemented
and guidelines provided in
COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND
JC No. 2015-01 dated January
8, 2015, on the prompt
submission of cash advance
voucher for Confidential Fund,
with covering transmittal letter
and supporting documents to the
AT; and (b) submit immediately
the requisite documents (b) Implemented
supporting the cash advance.

We recommended that the LCE
require the (a) members of the
Municipal Development
Council and MPDO to (i)
conduct adequate planning and
close monitoring of the
utilization of the fund to attain
substantial accomplishment; and
(ii) submit revised and/or
supplemental AIP to cover any
amendments in the planned
PPAs, if any; (b) MEO to (i)
immediately submit a written
satisfactory explanation
supported with complete
documentation on the noted
inconsistencies on the projects
“Upgrading of Juego Deusta
Alley and Francisco Dionisio
Street” in Caingin and
“Upgrading of Drainage Canal”
at P. Burgos Street in
Bagumbayan; (ii) monitor the
implementation of infrastructure
projects to ensure the
contractor’s compliance with
approved plans within the
scheduled completion time; and
(iii) include only necessary pay
items in the planning of each
project; (c) members of the
BAC and its Technical Working
Group to (i) conduct sufficient
bid evaluation and post
qualification to minimize the
occurrence of overlapping
projects and ensure the bidder’s
responsiveness to the plans and
POW; and (ii) conform with the
use of the prescribed format of
bid form; and (d) MA to (i)
ensure complete documentation
prior to processing of payment;
and (ii) require the contractors
to post surety bond or other
accepted forms prior to the
release of retention money in
accordance with the RIRR of
RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE
through the Municipal SWM
Board (a) expedite the
completion/updating of the ten-
year Municipal SWM Plan for
approval by the NSWMC to
serve as a guide for the efficient
management of solid waste
generated within the
Municipality’s jurisdiction; (b)
provide resources to fully
operate the Central MRF which
would reduce the residual waste
being hauled and collected by
the service provider; (c) enforce
strictly the sanctions for
violators of solid waste
management ordinances, laws
and regulations; and (d) further
intensify and involve the
barangay governments in
monitoring the compliance of
their constituents with
environmental laws, rules and
ordinances.

We recommended that the LCE
require the Human Resources
Management Officer and MA to
continue to comply with RA No.
7875, as amended by RA Nos.
9241 and 10606 and its RIRR
on the withholding and full
remittance of PhilHealth
contributions within the
prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE
instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (a) require the
concerned Accountable Officers
and employees to immediately
liquidate the outstanding cash
advances; (b); stop the practice
of granting cash advances to
personnel not designated as
Disbursing Officer by the Head
of the Agency; (c) desist from
the practice of transferring cash
advances from one accountable
officer to a non-bonded
implementing officer/personnel; and (d) draw cash advances only
when it is impractical to pay the
same by check.

We reiterated our
recommendation that the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) require
the (a) GSO to (i) conduct the
physical count of inventories
and submit the report thereon to
the Audit Team for verification;
(ii) prepare and submit the RIS
which should be consolidated in
the SSMI to serve as the
documentary basis for the
recording of issued supplies and
materials; (iii) provide a
centralized storeroom for storing
supplies and materials and
monitor properly the receipt and
issuance of inventories; and (b)
GSO and MAO Officers to
maintain SCs and SLCs,
respectively, for recording,
monitoring and reconciliation of
receipts and issuances of Inventory
accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous
year’s recommendations that the
LCE (a) direct the GSO, MT
and MA to obtain the
supporting documents
evidencing the ownership of the
recorded 12 parcels of land
totaling ₱19,878,274.34 and
facilitate the immediate
registration and titling of the
lots under the name of the
Municipality; (b) instruct the
MA to recognize in the books
the 23 unrecorded parcels of
land once the TCT’s are issued
under the name of the
Municipality on the basis of the
assessed /appraised value/s as
prescribed in IPSAS 17; (c)
create a Committee on
Inventory to conduct the annual
physical inventory of PPE and
avail of the “One-Time”
Cleansing of PPE accounts; and
(d) require the MA and GSO to
maintain PPELC and PC for
each category of PPE.

We recommended that the (a)
members of the SB refrain from
passing resolutions that are
contrary to the provisions of
law; and (b) Local Chief
Executive (LCE) require the
MDRRMO to (i) desist from
incurring ineligible expenditures
charged against the DRRMF to
serve the intended purpose of
the fund; and (ii) observe the
implementation of preparedness
and mitigation PPAs as listed in
the AIP to achieve the objective
of establishing a disaster-
resilient community and
enhance disaster preparedness at
all levels.

We recommended that the LCE
require (a) the Municipal
Engineering Office (MEO) to (i)
submit the complete detailed
engineering documents of all
PPAs; (ii) follow the guidelines
and standards set forth in the
preparation/ computation of
POW; (iii) exclude the 1%
Construction Engineering Cost”
in the computation of Program
of Work (iv) require the
contractor to post the
signboard/billboards containing
relevant information about the
project at all venues where the
PPAs will be implemented in
accordance with the full
disclosure policy of the
government; (v) initiate penal
sanctions against the contractors
that do not comply with the
requirements of POW, ABC and
contract agreement; (vi) require
the contractor to submit the
“As-Built” plan of completed
projects, reviewed and certified
by the Municipal Engineer ; and
(vii) ensure compliance by
Contractors with the technical
specifications of construction
materials and observance of
occupational health and safety
measures as provided in the
contract; and (b) the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to (i)
prepare the ABC on the basis of
the bidding documents for the
projects duly approved by the
Head of the Agency which
contain the same work items
and quantities as those to be
used by the contractors in
preparing their bid; and (c) the
Municipal Accountant to ensure
that documents are sufficient
before payment to the contractor
is made.

We recommended that the LCE
(a) require the (a) GSO Officer and
MSWDO to submit the lacking
information to substantiate the
reasonableness of the
procurement of food packs; (b)
GSO Officer to submit the
approved SO issued to the
supplier for the suspension of
delivery and proof of deliveries
for the alleged delivered items;
and (c) the Municipal
Accountant to deduct the
liquidated damages from the
payment due the supplier for
failure to fully deliver the items
within the agreed schedule, if
warranted.

We recommended that the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) require
the SB members to provide the
Audit Team their legal basis for
approving the payment of
Christmas incentives to the
Municipality’s elected officials,
coterminous, regular employees
and job order (JO) personnel.

We reiterated our
recommendations and the LCE
agreed to require the GSO
Officer who is responsible for
accepting deliveries to (a)
notify the Audit Team of the time
and date of the scheduled
deliveries for ocular inspection
to determine their existence and
condition at the time of
delivery; and (b) submit copies
of PO and within the prescribed
period.

We recommended that the LCE
require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to (a) immediately
remit the unremitted taxes
withheld in the amount of
₱24,394.46 to the BIR; and (b)
implement strictly the intact
remittance of taxes withheld to
the BIR within the prescribed
period to avoid the incurrence of
surcharges and penalties to be
charged against the erring
employees.

We recommended that the LCE
require the Chairperson of the
TWG-GFPS to (a) prepare the
Municipality’s GBP and
accomplishment report in
accordance with the above
regulations; (b) streamline PPAs
that promote gender responsive
governance, protect and fulfill
women’s human rights, promote
women’s economic
empowerment and gender
equality in the locality; and (c)
fully comply with all the
provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01 or
Guidelines on the Localization of
the Magna Carta of Women.

recorded obligations are valid

responsible to fast track the
implementation of the project
funded by the DBM for the

beneficiaries.

date.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations and the Management agreed and committed to  (a) form an inventory committee that will conduct an annual count of all  PPE items including the preparation and submission of the inventory report/RPCPPE; (b) direct the Accountant to exert extra effort in tracing/retrieving the accounting records to determine the details of PPE amounting to P11,023,298.66; (c) instruct the Accountant to recompute the depreciation in accordance with the schedule of useful life per COA Circular No. 2003-007 and draw the appropriate correcting journal entry; (d) instruct the Accountant to reclassify the PPE accounts to their proper accounts; and (e) direct the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort in retrieving the supporting documents evidencing ownership of the recorded parcel of lands and facilitate the immediate registration and titling of the several  lots in the name of the municipality.

No. 7160 for lack of legal basis; (b) require the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating claims without the available appropriations for the purpose; otherwise, require the Sangguniang Bayan to issue resolution for the realignment/issuance of supplemental of budget;   (c) advise the Municipal Accountant to see to it that claims for the membership, dues, and contributions be substantiated with the necessary budget and approved plans and programs as basis for the amount to be paid; and (d) advise the Municipal Treasurer that payments for the purpose made to duly recognized leagues and federations under the law be acknowledged with the prescribed government Accountable Form No. 51 or official receipts duly registered with the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

We recommended that (a) the accounting unit should see to it that payrolls are supported with complete documentations before effecting payment; (b) the HRMO to compute the payroll for actual services rendered and require the refund of any overpayment; (c) the HRMO to issue job order contracts with specific work duties to be undertaken; (d) the Municipal Accountant record the expenses incurred in the correct accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2004-008 dated September 20, 2004;  and (e) the Management consider the reduction of job order personnel to what is actually needed to save on operating costs.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials to submit justifications for the improper liquidation documents that did not reflect the validity of the undertaking for which the cash advance was intended.  We further recommend that the accountant should exercise prudence in the processing of claims to ensure legality and regularity of disbursements.

We recommended that Management (a) institute internal rules to regulate the subscription and assignment of cellular phones and expenses thereto, considering factors such as rank, cost and usage requirement of the position; and (b) practice prudence and economy on the use of government funds for mobile phone expenses.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) maintain special accounts for each economic enterprise in the General Fund; (b) operate and manage the corporate economic enterprises separate and distinct from its purely governmental/public functions; (c) segregate the assets, liabilities and equity of the economic enterprises from the General Fund proper; (d) manage these assets prudently to generate additional source of revenues; (e) identify and maintain appropriate property subsidiary ledger cards; and (f) prepare the financial reports for each of the economic enterprises.

We recommended and the Management agreed to  (a) prioritize the budget for 20% IRA on projects that would create greater impact in terms of sustainability; (b) advise the Local Development Council and the municipal officials concerned to prepare the development plan strictly in accordance with the guidelines of the DILG and carry out the targeted activities within the time frame; and (c) justify the realignment of the P5,740,215.00intended for development projects to Assistance to Regular Education Training, a clear violation of the Joint Circular.

We recommended the Management to (a) submit documents to the Audit Team for our evaluation to prove that the said seminar have been conducted as planned; (b) direct the BAC to submit explanations for the deficiencies noted in the bidding documents and processes; and(c) file appropriate action against the said supplier for submitting falsified documents.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management  (a) assign checkers to inspect/witness the weighing of residual wastes at the designated sanitary landfill; (b) require checkers to prepare daily accomplishment reports showing the weights/volumes of residual wastes hauled by the contractor; (c) submit additional documents to prove the correctness of the amount billed by RM Maintenance; (d) require the RM Maintenance to refund the amount P620,699.40 for excess payment by the Municipality; (e) invite the afore-named contractors to participate in the bidding for the contract on solid waste hauling services; (f) impose disciplinary action/sanctions to personnel who are responsible for the excessive payments to the contractor; and (g) study the possibility of purchasing its own dump trucks to haul the wastes to the sanitary landfill in San Jose del Monte City which is much nearer than Norzagaray to save on operating costs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, the Accountant and the Treasurer continue to cause the settlement of the outstanding suspensions and disallowances and be more prudent in the exercise of their functions in order to minimize, if not eliminate, audit suspensions, disallowances and charges in the future.
